prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.98) and high seasonality variance (0.808), with early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differing significantly from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/DWT_rf variants). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, up, up), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses.

The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, up, up), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses."

raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.174 shows moderate disagreement but not extreme (≥0.25 threshold). RMSE spread ratio=1.98 between best (THETA=3185) and worst (ONLY_FT_svr=11756) models justifies top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.808) and mixed trend directions. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.25 in inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting while still leveraging model performance signals."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.98) and high seasonality variance (0.808), with early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differing significantly from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/DWT_rf variants). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (down, up, up), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.174 shows moderate disagreement but not extreme (≥0.25 threshold). RMSE spread ratio=1.98 between best (THETA=3185) and worst (ONLY_FT_svr=11756) models justifies top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.808) and mixed trend directions. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.25 in inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting while still leveraging model performance signals.
